verse_context_groups.label: Inner deliberative reasoning and questioning (dialogizomai/dialogismos)
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names inner deliberative reasoning — questioning in the heart, calculating political consequences, reasoning within oneself in an interior monologue, and the evil thoughts that arise from the heart and produce sinful action. Jesus regularly perceives and names this inner deliberating; the dialogismos of the heart is the site of both corrupt reasoning and honest questioning. Inner-being phenomenon: inner deliberative reasoning.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Luk 5:22 When Jesus perceived their thoughts , he answered them , “ Why do you question in your hearts ?
anchor_verse.meaning: dialogizomai names the questioning in the heart that Jesus perceives — the inner deliberative reasoning that has not yet been spoken. Both dialogizomai and dialogismos appear in this verse.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'And immediately Jesus, perceiving in his spirit that they thus questioned within themselves, said to them, Why do you question these things in your hearts?' The phenomenon at its clearest: inner deliberative reasoning (dialogizomai) located explicitly in the heart, perceived by Jesus in his spirit, named and challenged. The verse makes the inner activity, its location, and its perceptibility to Christ all explicit simultaneously.

verses.verse_text: Mat 16:7 And they began discussing it among themselves , saying , “We brought no bread .”
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the communal inner reasoning about a practical matter — discussion among themselves as a form of shared deliberation.

verses.verse_text: Mat 16:8 But Jesus , aware of this, said , “O you of little faith , why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you have no bread ?
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the disciples' anxious shared reasoning — Jesus perceives their collective deliberation and challenges it.

verses.verse_text: Mat 21:25 The baptism of John , from where did it come ? From heaven or from man ?” And they discussed it among themselves , saying , “ If we say , ‘ From heaven ,’ he will say to us , ‘ Why then did you not believe him ?’
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the calculating shared inner reasoning of the leaders — they reason through the political consequences of each answer.

verses.verse_text: Mar 2:6 Now some of the scribes were sitting there , questioning in their hearts ,
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the scribes' inner silent reasoning — questioning in the heart as an inner deliberative act not yet spoken.

verses.verse_text: Mar 2:8 And immediately Jesus , perceiving in his spirit that they thus questioned within themselves , said to them , “ Why do you question these things in your hearts ?
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the inner questioning that Jesus perceives — the deliberative reasoning within the heart that challenges the healing-authority claim.

verses.verse_text: Mar 8:16 And they began discussing with one another the fact that they had no bread .
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the disciples' shared anxious reasoning — again about bread, the same failure as Mat 16:7.

verses.verse_text: Mar 9:33 And they came to Capernaum . And when he was in the house he asked them , “ What were you discussing on the way ?”
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the shared deliberation the disciples had on the road — they had been reasoning about who was greatest.

verses.verse_text: Luk 1:29 But she was greatly troubled at the saying , and tried to discern what sort of greeting this might be .
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names Mary's inner deliberative effort to understand the angel's greeting — the heart working to make sense of what has been said.

verses.verse_text: Luk 3:15 As the people were in expectation , and all were questioning in their hearts concerning John , whether he might be the Christ ,
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the inner deliberation of the expectant crowd — reasoning within themselves about John's identity as they awaited the coming one.

verses.verse_text: Luk 5:21 And the scribes and the Pharisees began to question , saying , “ Who is this who speaks blasphemies ? Who can forgive sins but God alone ?”
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the inner questioning of the religious leaders — the beginning of their deliberative challenge to Jesus's authority.

verses.verse_text: Luk 5:22 When Jesus perceived their thoughts , he answered them , “ Why do you question in your hearts ?
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the questioning in the heart that Jesus perceives — the inner deliberative reasoning that has not yet been spoken. Both dialogizomai and dialogismos appear in this verse.

verses.verse_text: Luk 12:17 and he thought to himself , ‘ What shall I do , for I have nowhere to store my crops ?’
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the rich man's inner deliberation with himself — reasoning within, presented as interior monologue.

verses.verse_text: Luk 20:14 But when the tenants saw him , they said to themselves , ‘ This is the heir . Let us kill him , so that the inheritance may be ours .’
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the tenants' inner deliberation — reasoning among themselves that produces a murderous decision.

verses.verse_text: Mat 15:19 For out of the heart come evil thoughts , murder , adultery , sexual immorality , theft , false witness , slander .
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the evil inner reasoning/thoughts that originate in the heart — these are not idle thoughts but the corrupt deliberative movements of the inner person that produce sinful action.

verses.verse_text: Mar 7:21 For from within , out of the heart of man , come evil thoughts , sexual immorality , theft , murder , adultery ,
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the evil inner reasoning/deliberating from the heart — the source is within; the thoughts precede and generate the actions. Same theology as Mat 15:19, Markan parallel.

verses.verse_text: Luk 2:35 ( and a sword will pierce through your own soul also ), so that thoughts from many hearts may be revealed .”
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner reasonings/deliberations of many hearts that will be exposed by the child — the hidden inner opinions and doubts that the light of Christ will surface.

verses.verse_text: Luk 5:22 When Jesus perceived their thoughts , he answered them , “ Why do you question in your hearts ?
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner thought-content that Jesus perceives — the formed inner deliberations that his knowing grasps.

verses.verse_text: Luk 6:8 But he knew their thoughts , and he said to the man with the withered hand , “ Come and stand here .” And he rose and stood there.
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner reasonings of the Pharisees about the Sabbath healing — their deliberative inner questioning is known to Jesus before they speak.

verses.verse_text: Luk 9:46 An argument arose among them as to which of them was the greatest .
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner disputed reasoning that becomes outward argument — the disciples' deliberating about status surfaces as communal controversy.

verses.verse_text: Luk 9:47 But Jesus , knowing the reasoning of their hearts , took a child and put him by his side
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner deliberative reasoning of the disciples — Jesus knows it and answers it with an enacted parable.

verses.verse_text: Luk 24:38 And he said to them , “ Why are you troubled , and why do doubts arise in your hearts ?
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner questioning/doubting of the disciples before the risen Jesus — the deliberative inner uncertainty that the resurrection appearance confronts.

verses.verse_text: Rom 1:21 For although they knew God , they did not honor him as God or give thanks to him, but they became futile in their thinking , and their foolish hearts were darkened .
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the corrupt inner reasoning that produces futility — the deliberative thought-life of those who knew God but did not honour him becomes empty.

verses.verse_text: Rom 14:1 As for the one who is weak in faith , welcome him , but not to quarrel over opinions .
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the disputed inner reasonings about food and days — the deliberative inner doubts and differing views that should not become the grounds of conflict.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 3:20 and again , “The Lord knows the thoughts of the wise , that they are futile .”
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner reasonings of the wise that God sees as futile — human deliberative cleverness under divine scrutiny is empty.

verses.verse_text: Phili 2:14 Do all things without grumbling or disputing ,
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner disputed reasoning/questioning that produces grumbling — what begins as an inner deliberative challenge surfaces as contention.

verses.verse_text: 1Ti 2:8 I desire then that in every place the men should pray , lifting holy hands without anger or quarreling ;
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner disputing/quarreling from which prayer must be free — what the hands do in prayer must not be accompanied by inner deliberative controversy.

verses.verse_text: Jam 2:4 have you not then made distinctions among yourselves and become judges with evil thoughts ?
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the evil inner reasoning that produces unjust discrimination — the deliberative inner evaluation of persons by appearance is named as evil.

verse_context_groups.label: Inner reflection and conceived thought (enthumeomai/enthumēsis)
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names sustained inner reflection that produces conceived thoughts — Joseph contemplating his situation before the angel appears, Peter pondering the vision before the Spirit speaks, the Pharisees thinking evil in their hearts, and the formed inner thoughts that the word of God discerns. The phenomenon is the inner conceiving activity that generates thought-content. Inner-being phenomenon: inner reflective conceiving of thought.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Heb 4:12 For the word of God is living and active , sharper than any two-edged sword , piercing to the division of soul and of spirit , of joints and of marrow , and discerning the thoughts and intentions of the heart .
anchor_verse.meaning: enthumēsis names the inner conceived thoughts that the word of God discerns — the cognitive acts of the heart are among the inner realities the living word penetrates and exposes.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'Discerning the thoughts and intentions of the heart.' The word of God penetrates to the enthumēseis (conceived thoughts) and intentions of the heart. This verse names the phenomenon directly: the thoughts that are conceived in the heart are the inner realities that the living word reaches. The dynamic (word penetrating to conceived thoughts) reveals what the term names — formed inner conceptions.

verses.verse_text: Mat 1:20 But as he considered these things , behold , an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream , saying , “ Joseph , son of David , do not fear to take Mary as your wife , for that which is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit .
verses.meaning: enthumeomai names Joseph's sustained inner reflection on the situation with Mary — the deliberate inner attending to a perplexing moral situation that is the context into which the angel speaks.

verses.verse_text: Mat 9:4 But Jesus , knowing their thoughts , said , “ Why do you think evil in your hearts ?
verses.meaning: enthumeomai names the inner reflective-deliberative activity of the scribes — thinking/reasoning within themselves, which Jesus perceives and names.

verses.verse_text: Act 10:19 And while Peter was pondering the vision , the Spirit said to him , “ Behold , three men are looking for you .
verses.meaning: enthumeomai names Peter's sustained inner reflection on the vision — the deliberate attending to what he has seen that is the context into which the Spirit speaks.

verses.verse_text: Mat 9:4 But Jesus , knowing their thoughts , said , “ Why do you think evil in your hearts ?
verses.meaning: enthumēsis names the inner thought-content as what Jesus perceives — the conceived thoughts that his knowing grasps directly. Both enthumeomai (activity) and enthumēsis (content) appear in the same verse.

verses.verse_text: Mat 12:25 Knowing their thoughts , he said to them , “ Every kingdom divided against itself is laid waste , and no city or house divided against itself will stand .
verses.meaning: enthumēsis names the inner conceptions of the Pharisees that Jesus knows — the formed thoughts that arose in response to the healing.

verses.verse_text: Act 17:29 Being then God’s offspring , we ought not to think that the divine being is like gold or silver or stone , an image formed by the art and imagination of man .
verses.meaning: enthumēsis names the inner conception of God that human art embodies — the imaginative act of forming a conception of the divine is the target of Paul's critique. God is not the product of human inner conceiving.

verses.verse_text: Heb 4:12 For the word of God is living and active , sharper than any two-edged sword , piercing to the division of soul and of spirit , of joints and of marrow , and discerning the thoughts and intentions of the heart .
verses.meaning: enthumēsis names the inner conceived thoughts that the word of God discerns — the cognitive acts of the heart are among the inner realities the living word penetrates and exposes.

verse_context_groups.label: Sustained meditative dwelling (si.ach, si.chah, ha.gig, hig.ga.von)
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names the sustained inner activity of meditation — dwelling on God's precepts, pondering his mighty acts, musing until the heart burns, the heart's meditation offered as worship. The meditative inner life is directed toward God's word and works, producing understanding, praise, and orientation through distress. Inner-being phenomenon: sustained inward meditative dwelling.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Mem Psa 119:97 Oh how I love your law ! It is my meditation all the day .
anchor_verse.meaning: si.chah names all-day sustained meditative engagement with the law — the love for the law is expressed in and sustained by continuous inner dwelling upon it.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'Oh how I love your law! It is my meditation all the day.' Love for the law finds its expression and sustaining in si.chah — all-day meditative dwelling on what God has said. The verse names the object (the law), the quality (all-day continuousness), and the relational tone (love) of meditative dwelling. No other verse states the phenomenon so fully.

verses.verse_text: Judg 5:10 “Tell of it , you who ride on white donkeys , you who sit on rich carpets and you who walk by the way .
verses.meaning: Names the activity of those who sit and reflect — the term characterises travellers pausing to meditate or speak of what they have witnessed.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 16:9 Sing to him, sing praises to him; tell of all his wondrous works !
verses.meaning: si.ach names the expressive telling of God's wondrous acts — the inner musing poured outward in proclamation. The meditative attending to God's works produces the speech of praise.

verses.verse_text: Job 7:11 “ Therefore I will not restrain my mouth ; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit ; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul .
verses.meaning: si.ach names complaint as the outward expression of inner anguish — what the soul has been musingly dwelling on in bitterness is now spoken.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:8 or the bushes of the earth , and they will teach you; and the fish of the sea will declare to you.
verses.meaning: Names the act of inner reflection and communication — the term identifies the capacity of the earth's creatures to teach through their own musing or declaring.

verses.verse_text: Psa 55:17 Evening and morning and at noon I utter my complaint and moan , and he hears my voice .
verses.meaning: si.ach names the complaint that is the sustained inner expression of distress — three times daily the psalmist voices the musing of the burdened inner life.

verses.verse_text: Psa 69:12 I am the talk of those who sit in the gate , and the drunkards make songs about me.
verses.meaning: si.ach names the derisive talk of others — the psalmist's situation has become the subject of public musing and mockery. Here si.ach applies to others' inner dwelling turned hostile.

verses.verse_text: Psa 77:3 When I remember God , I moan ; when I meditate , my spirit faints . Selah
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative reflection that produces anguish rather than comfort — the inner dwelling on God's apparent absence intensifies distress.

verses.verse_text: Psa 77:6 I said, “Let me remember my song in the night ; let me meditate in my heart .” Then my spirit made a diligent search :
verses.meaning: si.ach names deliberate inner meditative attending in the night — the psalmist turns the mind inward to search for what God has done.

verses.verse_text: Psa 77:12 I will ponder all your work , and meditate on your mighty deeds .
verses.meaning: si.ach names sustained meditative attention to God's acts — the decision to dwell inwardly on what God has done as the path through distress.

verses.verse_text: Psa 105:2 Sing to him, sing praises to him; tell of all his wondrous works !
verses.meaning: si.ach names the telling of God's wondrous works — meditative inner attending turned to outward proclamation in praise. Same as 1Ch 16:9.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:15 I will meditate on your precepts and fix my eyes on your ways .
verses.meaning: si.ach names sustained meditative attention to God's precepts — the inner dwelling on the law that orients the eyes and the will toward God's ways.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:23 Even though princes sit plotting against me, your servant will meditate on your statutes .
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative devotion as the counter to external hostility — the inner dwelling on God's statutes is maintained even under threat.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:27 Make me understand the way of your precepts , and I will meditate on your wondrous works .
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative engagement promised in response to understanding received — the psalmist will dwell inwardly on God's wonders when given the capacity to understand.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:48 I will lift up my hands toward your commandments , which I love , and I will meditate on your statutes .
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative inner activity alongside physical gesture — both the lifted hands and the inner musing express devotion to the commandments.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:78 Let the insolent be put to shame, because they have wronged me with falsehood ; as for me , I will meditate on your precepts .
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative devotion as the psalmist's response to the insolent — while they act in falsehood, the inner life is sustained by dwelling on God's precepts.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:148 My eyes are awake before the watches of the night , that I may meditate on your promise .
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative attention to God's promise as the reason for wakefulness — the inner dwelling on what God has said sustains the night-vigil.

verses.verse_text: Psa 143:5 I remember the days of old ; I meditate on all that you have done ; I ponder the work of your hands .
verses.meaning: si.ach names the pondering of God's past acts — sustained inner attending to divine works as the means of finding orientation in present distress.

verses.verse_text: Psa 145:5 On the glorious splendor of your majesty , and on your wondrous works , I will meditate .
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative dwelling on divine majesty and works — inner attending as an act of sustained worship and wonder.

verses.verse_text: Pro 6:22 When you walk , they will lead you; when you lie down , they will watch over you; and when you awake , they will talk with you .
verses.meaning: si.ach names the wisdom's inner conversation with the person who has received it — parental wisdom becomes a speaking inner companion, musing with the person upon waking.

verses.verse_text: Isa 53:8 By oppression and judgment he was taken away ; and as for his generation , who considered that he was cut off out of the land of the living , stricken for the transgression of my people ?
verses.meaning: si.ach names the inner meditative attending that no one directed toward the Servant's death — his generation did not dwell on or consider the meaning of what was happening to him.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:4 But you are doing away with the fear of God and hindering meditation before God .
verses.meaning: si.chah names sustained inner meditative attending before God as what Job's words are accused of undermining — meditation before God is a defining form of the inner life that Eliphaz considers Job to be threatening.

verses.verse_text: Mem Psa 119:97 Oh how I love your law ! It is my meditation all the day .
verses.meaning: si.chah names all-day sustained meditative engagement with the law — the love for the law is expressed in and sustained by continuous inner dwelling upon it.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:99 I have more understanding than all my teachers , for your testimonies are my meditation .
verses.meaning: si.chah names testimonies-meditation as what produces understanding surpassing human teachers — the sustained inner engagement with God's word produces more than external instruction.

verses.verse_text: Psa 49:3 My mouth shall speak wisdom ; the meditation of my heart shall be understanding .
verses.meaning: ha.gut names heart-meditation as the activity that produces understanding — the reflective inner engagement of the mind before God is what understanding is made of.

verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster : for the flutes . A Psalm of David . Psa 5:1 Give ear to my words , O Lord ; consider my groaning .
verses.meaning: ha.gig names inner musing that has become groaning — deep reflective engagement with distress that the psalmist presents to God as the substance of prayer.

verses.verse_text: Psa 39:3 My heart became hot within me. As I mused , the fire burned ; then I spoke with my tongue :
verses.meaning: ha.gig names the deep inner reflective engagement that generates heat within — musing that intensifies to burning and finally breaks out as speech.

verses.verse_text: Psa 19:14 Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in your sight , O Lord , my rock and my redeemer .
verses.meaning: hig.ga.von names the heart's meditation as what is offered to God alongside the words of the mouth — the inner reflective activity is the inward counterpart to outward speech. Both are offered as worship.

verses.verse_text: Lam 3:62 The lips and thoughts of my assailants are against me all the day long.
verses.meaning: hig.ga.von names the hostile meditations/thoughts of the assailants — what they dwell on inwardly and utter with their lips is turned against the suffering person.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:27 Make me understand the way of your precepts , and I will meditate on your wondrous works .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner faculty the psalmist asks God to open — petitionary seeking of understanding for the precepts.

verses.verse_text: Psa 49:3 My mouth shall speak wisdom ; the meditation of my heart shall be understanding .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as what the heart's meditation produces — the inner reflective activity generates understanding as its fruit.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:99 I have more understanding than all my teachers , for your testimonies are my meditation .
verses.meaning: Names the inner attainment the speaker claims through meditation on God's testimonies — the term identifies the surplus of understanding that comes from attending to divine instruction.

verse_context_groups.label: Contemplation in vision and divine knowledge of human thought
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names either Daniel's focused contemplative attention directed at the revelatory vision (se.khal), or the divine knowing and declaring of what is in the creature's thought (se.ach in Amos 4:13). The phenomenon is either the intense contemplative attending of the person in a revelatory context, or the divine penetrating awareness of human inner thought. Inner-being phenomenon: concentrated contemplation or divine attending to inner thought.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Dan 7:8 I considered the horns , and behold , there came up among them another horn , a little one, before which three of the first horns were plucked up by the roots . And behold , in this horn were eyes like the eyes of a man , and a mouth speaking great things .
anchor_verse.meaning: se.khal names Daniel's focused contemplative attention directed at the vision — a deliberate inner act of attending closely to what he sees in the revelatory vision, producing discernment of its elements.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'I considered the horns, and behold, there came up among them another horn.' Daniel's se.khal is a focused, active inner attending directed at the vision — each element is being contemplated in turn. The verb names not passive reception but engaged inner attending that notices, attends, and discerns within the revelatory space.

verses.verse_text: Amo 4:13 For behold , he who forms the mountains and creates the wind , and declares to man what is his thought , who makes the morning darkness , and treads on the heights of the earth — the Lord , the God of hosts , is his name !
verses.meaning: se.ach names the divine knowledge of human thought — God knowing and declaring what is in the person's inner meditative life. The creative God who forms mountains also knows the inner thought of the creature.

verses.verse_text: Dan 7:8 I considered the horns , and behold , there came up among them another horn , a little one, before which three of the first horns were plucked up by the roots . And behold , in this horn were eyes like the eyes of a man , and a mouth speaking great things .
verses.meaning: se.khal names Daniel's focused contemplative attention directed at the vision — a deliberate inner act of attending closely to what he sees in the revelatory vision, producing discernment of its elements.

verse_context_groups.label: Set aside — no inner-being phenomenon
verse_context_groups.context_description: Psa 92:3 where hig.ga.von names the sound of a musical instrument, not an inner meditative activity.

verses.verse_text: Psa 92:3 to the music of the lute and the harp , to the melody of the lyre .
verses.meaning: hig.ga.von set aside as no inner-being content — musical instrument sound, not meditative activity.
